prompt:
Our log shipper tails NDJSON files and the current line splitting is a disgrace — it does chunk.split('\n') per read, so any JSON record that straddles a read boundary gets shipped as two broken half-lines, and Windows-produced files leave a \r glued onto the end of every record. I ripped it out. Please write the replacement properly in lines.ts:

export an async generator splitLines(chunks) where chunks is an AsyncIterable<string> or a plain Iterable<string>, yielding one complete line at a time with the terminator removed.

Rules: \n and \r\n both end a line (a \r\n split across two chunks is still ONE terminator); a \r on its own is just content, not a line ending; blank lines count and come through as ''; if the stream ends mid-line the partial line is yielded last; if it ends exactly on a terminator there's no phantom empty line. Empty chunks can appear anywhere and mean nothing. And it must actually stream — emit each line as soon as its chunk has arrived, don't slurp the whole source (we tail files that never end).

Contract is test_lines.ts, run with `node --test test_lines.ts`, hands off the test file. Zero dependencies.
